title: Cavendishia hendersonii
taxonomy: Cavendishia hendersonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Cavendishia, which is part of the family Ericaceae. This plant is native to South America and is a type of shrub or small tree. The classification of Cavendishia hendersonii is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Cavendishia hendersonii is considered a rare and unique species within the Ericaceae family.

intro: Cavendishia hendersonii, commonly known as the 'Henderson's Cavendishia', is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
